content:
# RAUNE-Net + LAB Grade: Best Color Result So Far (2026-04-01)

## Finding
RAUNE-Net + shadow-aware LAB grade produces the strongest "warm subjects on blue water" look of any model/grading combination tested. R/G ratio hits 0.97-1.00 (near neutral), significantly better than WaterNet+LAB (0.86-0.91) or RAUNE+Ambiance (0.92-0.93).

## LAB Grade v2 Parameters (tuned for RAUNE-Net's darker output)
- contrast=1.15 (lower than WaterNet's 1.4 — RAUNE already has more contrast)
- saturation=1.3
- warmth_a=5.0, warmth_b=3.0
- shadow_lift=0.05 (prevents black crush on RAUNE-Net's darker output)
- S-curve centered on image mean (not 0.5) — critical for RAUNE-Net's darker distribution

## Full Comparison (averaged across 3 frames)

| Metric | WaterNet+LAB | RAUNE+Ambiance | RAUNE+LAB grade |
|--------|-------------|----------------|-----------------|
| R/G ratio | 0.878 | 0.930 | **0.981** |
| B/G ratio | 1.147 | 1.130 | 1.113 |
| Saturation | 0.310 | 0.260 | 0.281 |
| White clip | 2.78% | 0.11% | 5.30% |
| Black clip | 2.31% | 0.02% | 2.63% |

## Key Insight
RAUNE-Net's better base red recovery (R/G 0.877 vs WaterNet 0.864) compounds with the LAB grade's warm shift. The LAB grade adds ~0.10 to R/G ratio on both models, but RAUNE-Net starts from a higher baseline, landing near 1.0 (neutral).

## Tradeoff
5.3% white clipping in bright areas (water surface, bubbles). Ambiance approach (0.11% clipping) is safer but less punchy. May be addressable with depth-aware grading — apply less contrast/warmth to highlights and distant/bright water.

## Next Step
Explore depth-context-aware enhancement: use Depth Anything V2 depth maps to apply graduated grading — stronger warm shift on near subjects (divers), gentler on far/bright background water. This should reduce white clipping while preserving the warm subject look.
